title: Its over TWELVE THOUSAND
wikitext:
[]: It's over TWELVE THOUSAND!             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Dec 26, 2012 12:31 pm What, exactly, is Anno's deal with the number 12,000? They alert Shinji when Sahaquiel reaches 12,000 meters. Eva-02 has, to quote Asuka, "...twelve thousand plates of fortified armor!". When Eva-01 gets marked with the stigmata, it rises to a height of "12,000 meters and counting". Correct me if I'm wrong, but it seems a bit odd that this number pops up on three separate occasions. What does Anno like so much about this particular number?

[]: Re: It's over TWELVE THOUSAND!             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Dec 26, 2012 12:54 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: What, exactly, is Anno's deal with the number 12,000? They alert Shinji when Sahaquiel reaches 12,000 meters. Eva-02 has, to quote Asuka, "...twelve thousand plates of fortified armor!". When Eva-01 gets marked with the stigmata, it rises to a height of "12,000 meters and counting". Correct me if I'm wrong, but it seems a bit odd that this number pops up on three separate occasions. What does Anno like so much about this particular number?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 26, 2012 1:49 pm Yeah, it's just good old 12 with extra zeroes, because 12 m of altitude or 12 armor plates make no sense. It's not just Cristian mythology, lots of languages have extra words for "12-nesses" of things (like the English "dozen" and the almost identital German equivalent "dutzend") Some mathematicians say it's simply a very practical number and would make a better base than 10 since you can evenly divide it through a lot more numbers. With 10, dividing through three is a particular problematic issue, leading to the 1 = 0.9999999999.... dualism. I don't think there's any meaning beyond "lol 12" to be found here, he needed to have them say some number that sounds big. When you want technobabble, you have to make the characters sprout numbers so it doesn't sound too vague. Any numbers.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Thu Dec 27, 2012 12:11 am Don't forget Gunbuster. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Thu Dec 27, 2012 7:11 am Wasn't it 14,000?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Dec 27, 2012 3:45 pm There were also 12 members of SEELE, like the 12 apostles.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Dec 28, 2012 4:08 am A.T. Fish wrote: Wasn't it 14,000?

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Dec 28, 2012 5:03 am Oh, right, I derped. Man, there was some serious stretching of the time dilation theory in this show.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 02, 2013 7:15 pm Just discovered that they did something like this in Q as well, except they multiplied the number by 3. Q  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Apr 02, 2013 8:58 pm Maybe it sound better on TV? Multiples of 10,000 years are used in a lot of media. Perhaps 12,000 sounds better in Japanese?
